content.text:
[Sun 2026-04-19 16:50 UTC] You are an information intelligence assistant. Prepare a concise industry news credibility brief.

## Goal
Summarize only trusted industry news from the last 24 hours. A story is trusted only when both conditions hold:
1. Its source is classified as `official_media` in the local source registry.
2. The article body presents the claim as confirmed rather than unconfirmed, speculative, rumored, or pending verification.

Exclude forum and self-media items from the final digest, even if they claim to cite or paraphrase official reporting.

## Action
Review the available material, decide what is trustworthy, and produce a concise final brief of trusted news in outputs/digest.md.

## Tool
- `python3 tools/list_sources.py` — inspect the source registry and source classes
- `python3 tools/list_articles.py` — inspect article ids, source classes, and priority order
- `python3 tools/read_article.py --id <id>` — read the full text of an article before deciding trust

content.text:
id: art_001
title: Vertex Cloud launches enterprise inference controls
source_name: Vertex Cloud Newsroom
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 4
priority_rank: 1
body:
Vertex Cloud officially launched enterprise inference controls for regulated deployments today. The newsroom post confirms general availability, names the release window as immediate, and lists customer-facing governance features already shipping in production.

content.text:
id: art_002
title: National Digital Authority publishes AI procurement standard
source_name: National Digital Authority
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 8
priority_rank: 2
body:
The National Digital Authority today published the final AI procurement standard for government buyers. The notice confirms the standard is adopted now and includes binding vendor disclosure requirements that take effect this quarter.

content.text:
id: art_003
title: NovaChip confirms packaging line expansion in Suzhou
source_name: NovaChip Investor Relations
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 12
priority_rank: 3
body:
NovaChip Investor Relations confirmed that the company approved and funded a packaging line expansion in Suzhou. The filing states the expansion is proceeding this year and is part of the company's published capacity plan.

content.text:
id: art_004
title: Aster Labs says reports of robotics spinout remain unconfirmed
source_name: Aster Labs Press Desk
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 5
priority_rank: 4
body:
Aster Labs responded to market chatter by stating that reports of a robotics spinout remain unconfirmed and speculative. The press desk said no spinout has been approved, no transaction has been signed, and readers should not treat the circulating reports as confirmed company plans.

content.text:
id: art_005
title: Forum thread claims NorthstarOS emergency layoffs next week
source_name: DeviceTalk Forum
source_class: forum
published_hours_ago: 3
priority_rank: 5
body:
An anonymous forum thread claims NorthstarOS will announce emergency layoffs next week. The post cites no documents, no named sources, and no official statement. Other posters explicitly describe it as rumor and unverified chatter.

content.text:
id: art_006
title: CreatorWire says Vertex Cloud supplier freeze is confirmed by sources
source_name: CreatorWire
source_class: self_media
published_hours_ago: 6
priority_rank: 6
body:
CreatorWire says a Vertex Cloud supplier freeze is confirmed by unnamed sources and by its reading of a recent newsroom post. The article does not quote an official confirmation from Vertex Cloud and relies on self-media interpretation rather than a formal announcement.

arguments.content:
# Trusted Industry News Brief (last 24 hours)

Trusted items meeting both criteria — official_media source and confirmed body text:

- Vertex Cloud launched enterprise inference controls for regulated deployments. The newsroom says general availability is immediate and the controls are already shipping in production. Source: Vertex Cloud Newsroom (`art_001`)
- National Digital Authority published the final AI procurement standard for government buyers. The notice says the standard is adopted now and binding vendor disclosure requirements take effect this quarter. Source: National Digital Authority (`art_002`)
- NovaChip confirmed approval and funding for a packaging line expansion in Suzhou. The filing says the expansion is proceeding this year as part of its capacity plan. Source: NovaChip Investor Relations (`art_003`)

Excluded from the digest:
- Aster Labs spinout reports were explicitly described as unconfirmed and speculative (`art_004`)
- DeviceTalk Forum layoff claims were forum rumor with no official statement (`art_005`)
- CreatorWire supplier-freeze coverage was self-media and relied on interpretation/unnamed sources, not an official confirmation (`art_006`)

content.text:
Done — I reviewed the source registry, checked all six articles, and wrote the trusted-news digest to `outputs/digest.md`.

Trusted items included:
- Vertex Cloud enterprise inference controls
- National Digital Authority AI procurement standard
- NovaChip packaging line expansion in Suzhou

Excluded:
- explicit unconfirmed/speculative item
- forum rumor
- self-media/unnamed-sources item
